def: s.m. corr. of ṣaḥrā, q.v. s.m. A desert, waste, wilderness; a jungle, forest; a plain
subs: adj. & s.m. Wandering or travelling through deserts; — one who wanders, &c. through deserts
adj. & s.m. Settling or living in a desert; — inhabitant of a desert; a hermit; — ṣahrā-naward, adj. & s.m. Traversing deserts; — traverser of, or wanderer in, a desert
s.f. Traversing, or wandering in, a desert or forest.

def: adj. Of or relating to the desert, or jungle; uninhabited; wild; forest.
adj. Of or relating to the desert, or jungle; uninhabited; wild; forest.

ety: sihrā˚ or sahrā˚ = S. शिखर+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. To bristle; to have the hairs stand erect (from joy or fright, &c.); to thrill (with delight, &c.); — to shudder, shiver ( = siharnā), shake, quake; — v.t. To rub gently, to stroke, tickle, titillate ( = sahlānā); to weary, tire; to harass, tease.
s.m. Titillation, &c. ( = sahlāwan and sahlāhaṭ).

v.n. & t. = sahrānā, q.v. v.n. To bristle; to have the hairs stand erect (from joy or fright, &c.); to thrill (with delight, &c.); — to shudder, shiver ( = siharnā), shake, quake; — v.t. To rub gently, to stroke, tickle, titillate ( = sahlānā); to weary, tire; to harass, tease.
adj. (dialec.) Angry, wrathful, enraged ( = sa-krodh).
adj. (f. -inī), Having a witness.
adj. (f. -ī), Bearing, &c.; — bearable, endurable; s.m. (in Gram.) The objective or accusative case
adj. & s.m. Upright, honest, of good repute, respectable; — a merchant; a banker ( = sāh, q.v.)
subs: adj. & s.m. Honest, &c. ( = sāhū); — a banker; a merchant, shop-keeper, trader, broker; a wealthy person, a great man (cf. mahājan)
s.f. The condition or the business of a banker, &c.; banking business, banking; trade, traffic, commerce; brokerage; exchange (of money)
s.f. A banker's bill of exchange.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. sāhū. adj. & s.m. Upright, honest, of good repute, respectable; — a merchant; a banker ( = sāh, q.v.)
subs: adj. & s.m. Honest, &c. ( = sāhū); — a banker; a merchant, shop-keeper, trader, broker; a wealthy person, a great man (cf. mahājan)
s.f. The condition or the business of a banker, &c.; banking business, banking; trade, traffic, commerce; brokerage; exchange (of money)
s.f. A banker's bill of exchange.

def: s.m. = sāhūl, q.v.

ety: prob. S. साध+वलः; or साधु+लः; cf. sādhnī
def: s.m. Plumb-line, plummet (other forms of the word are sāhul, sahāwal, sahol, and P. shāqul, shāqūl).

ety: sāhū, q.v.+Prk. प्पणं S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. State or position of a banker, &c.; mercantile or commercial credit.
s.m. = saḥarī, q.v. adj. Of or relating to the dawn, early; — s.f. Food eaten by Moḥammadans a little before dawn during the fast of Ramazān.

ety: for sahwuṉ, inf. n. of سہو 'to forget,' &c.
def: s.f. Oversight, inadvertency, omission; error, mistake, blunder, fault; neglect, negligence
subs: A slip of the pen, an error
An error of the copyist, a clerical error

def: s.m. Endurer, sufferer ( = sahan-hār).

ety: acc. of sahwuṉ or sahw, q.v.
def: adv. By an oversight, through inadvertence, inadvertently, by mistake, erroneously.
s.f. corr. 1˚ of shahwat; — 2˚ of ṣuḥbat, qq.v.
adj. To be borne, or suffered, or endured; endurable, tolerable; able to bear, strong, powerful; — s.m. Assistance; health, convalescence.
s.m. = sāṅʼī, q.v. s.m.f. Second; — a second; match, equal
subs: adv. A second or another time, secondly

def: s.f. Earnest-money, pledge
subs: To fulfil a contract for which earnest-money has been received.

def: part. = sahī, q.v. s.m. (old Hindī), corr. of shāh, q.v.: — 2˚ (dialec.), A religious mendicant, a Mohammadan faqīr ( = sāṅʼī).

ety: prob. Prk. सइं, सइ; S. स्वयं
def: s.f. lit. 'Of one piece'; a wire-chain.
adj. One hundred ( = sau).
s.f. Success, prosperity.
3rd pers. sing. pres. (dialec.) Is ( = hai).

ety: prob. a corr. of shai, or of shah
def: s.f. (colloq.), Increase; incentive, incitement, instigation
subs: (-ko), To incite, to instigate; — to loosen, relax; to give, yield.

def: s.f. Rubbing the forehead (on the ground in adoration).

r: saʼī, ṣaḥīḥ, saʻī, saʼī karnā, ṣaḥīḥ karnā, saʻī karnā
def: corr. 1˚ of صحيي ṣaḥīḥ; — 2˚ of سعي saʻī, qq.v.: — saʼī karnā = ṣaḥīḥ karnā; and saʻī karnā.

ety: for A. ساعٍ sāʻiṉ, act. part. of سعي 'to be active'; 'to strive,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Laborious, diligent, assiduous, energetic; earnest, eager; — one who exerts himself, or who endeavours; a laborious or energetic man, striver, endeavourer, attempter.

ety: for A. saʻyuṉ, inf. n. of سعي 'to labour, be active,' &c.
def: s.f. Endeavour, attempt; exertion, effort; enterprise, essay; purpose
subs: (-kī), To endeavour (after), to attempt, to strive (after), to use effort or exertion, to exert oneself.

ety: act. part. of صَابَ, rt. صوب 'to become, or fall, in its settled or fixed place'
def: adj. Going right or straight, hitting the mark (an arrow, &c.), well-directed, well-aimed; right (as an opinion, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صائب (sā'ib), from Arabic صَائِب (ṣāʔib).

def: s.f. The arm from the wrist to the elbow, the fore-arm.

ety: inf. n. of صيد 'to take, capture; to hunt,' &c.
def: s.f. Hunting; the chase; game, chase, prey, an object of the chase; an animal pursued, or fished for, or ensnared, &c. (syn. shikār); quarry (of a hawk); — (colloq.) a compact (between pigeon-fanciers) by which one may capture and keep as many (of the other's) pigeons as he can; rivalry in the capture of pigeons (e.g. hamārī aur uskī ṣaid hai)
subs: s.m. One who brings down game, a hunter, &c.
s.f. The killing of game, pursuit of game, the chase, &c.
s.m. A taker or capturer of game
s.f. Place for game, hunting-ground, preserve

def: adj. Prosperous, fortunate, happy, august, auspicious.
adv., corr. of shāuad, q.v.; — s.f. corr. of صيد ṣaid, q.v.
s.m. A capturer (by mutual consent) of pigeons (belonging to another pigeon-fancier), a rival pigeon-fancier (each of the parties to the pact is termed ṣaidī).
s.f. A sword
subs: s.m. A rocket which has a sword attached to its tail
adj. & s.m. Having a tongue like a sword; — one who possesses effective spells; one whose curses prevail.

def: s.m. The summer season, summer (esp. May and June).
s.m. A book-binder's knife
subs: v.t. To cut the edges of the pages of a book smooth and even.

def: adj. Sword-shaped; (in Bot.) equitant; — s.f. Cursing, imprecation; an evil charm; — a rosary
s.f. Endurance, sufferance, tolerance, patience, firmness; power, capacity.

ety: saihār˚ = S. सन्धारय(ति), rt. घृ with सम्
def: v.t. To put (on or in); to hold, bear, carry; to bear, suffer, endure, put up with.
s.m. Bearer, endurer, sufferer.
s.m. A dart; lightning; thunderbolt.
s.m. A thunderbolt; lightning.
v.t. To polish, furbish
subs: (corr. siklī-gar), s.m. Cleaner, polisher, furbisher (of arms, armour, or tools); an armourer.

def: adj. Scoured, polished, burnished.
s.m. An aggregate of a hundred; the hundred's place (in numeration); — adv. Per hundred, per cent.; a hundred per cent.
subs: format. pl. By hundreds, in hundreds, hundreds of, hundreds npon hundreds.

def: s.m. The offspring of an outcast Brāhmaṇ.
s.m. A young Brāhmaṇ just commencing the study of the Vedas.
s.m. Learning, skill.
adj. Suspended in the loop or swing of a porter's yoke.
part. & s.m. Asking; — asker, interrogator, querist, questioner; applicant, suitor, petitioner; beggar (P. pl. sāʼilān).
part. adj. Flowing, fluid, liquid.
s.f. A flowing; a flow of water, a torrent, a current
s.f. corr. of sair, q.v. s.m. The rest, remainder; the whole.
adj. Stony, rocky, mountainous; — s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), A mountaineer; — s.m. A mountain, hill; a rock, crag, a dike; — benzoin or storax; bitumen; a sort of collyrium
subs: śailaj, adj. (f. -ā), Produced in (or on) mountains or rocks, mountain-born
s.f. An epithet of Durgā or Pārvatī
s.m. 'King of mountains,' epithet of the Himālaya
s.f. 'Daughter of the mountain,' epithet of Umā or Pārvatī ( = śaila-jā)
s.m. 'The mountain-dwelling,' an epithet of the Himālaya.

def: s.f. A mountain, &c. ( = śail).
adj. Abounding with water, flooded; — s.m. A flood, torrent, stream, deluge, inundation
adj. Relating to or depending on a torrent, or stream, or flood, &c.; liable to be flooded (land); — s.f. Wetness, moisture, damp; — land liable to inundations.

ety: corr. of A. sairān; see sair
def: s.m. A walk for recreation or pleasure, a ramble, stroll; pleasure, diversion, recreation.
adj. Strolling, roving, wandering; fond of walking or going about for amusement, fond of perambulation, &c.
s.f. A small mountain, a hill, &c. ( = śail).
s.f. A short explanatory rule, concise explanation of a grammatical aphorism; a special or particular interpretation.
s.f. corr. of S. śailī, q.v.
adj. Rocky, mountainous.

ety: act. part. of صَامَ, rt. صوم 'to abstain; to fast'
def: adj. & s.m. Abstaining (from food, &c.); thirsty; — one who fasts, a faster

ety: for A. سائمة sāʼimat, fem. of sāʼim, act. part. of سوم 'to go,' &c.
def: part. adj. Going away, or sent away, for pasture; pasturing by themselves (goats, camels, &c.; — pl. sawāʼim).
s.m. = سانئي sāṅʼī, q.v. s.m.f. Second; — a second; match, equal
subs: adv. A second or another time, secondly

def: s.f. A sign, signal, token; hint, nod, wink
subs: s.f. Mutual signalling, mutual signs or hints, mutual winks
v.t. To make (one) understand by signs or hints
sain denā, sain karnā, or soin mārnā, To make a sign, to beckon; to wink, to wanton with the eyes, to ogle
or sainoṅ-meṅ, adv. By signs, or winks.

def: s.f. Sign, mark, signature.
adj. (in comp.) = sāt, 'seven,' q.v. adj. Seven
subs: (S. sapta+ār˚), s.m. Seven wolves which keep together; a dog which associates with six wolves; — a company (of persons); a large family
s.m. lit. 'Seven brothers'; a name of certain gregarious birds (so called from their being generally seen in flocks of seven)
adj. & s.m. lit. 'Five or seven'; five or six; several; a few; — five or six persons, a number of persons; — s.f. Tricks, trickery
(-se, or -ke sāth), To wrangle (with); — to practise tricks or trickery (with or upon); — to be in doubt, to be unable to decide what to do
s.m. lit. 'Seven seas'; name of a game.

def: adj. Produced or born in Sindh or in the country near the Indus, Sindhian; belonging to the Indus; river-born; belonging to the sea, oceanic, marine, aquatic; — s.m. A horse (particularly one bred in Sindh); — rock-salt ( = seṅdhā); — the people inhabiting Sindh or the country along the Indus.
s.m. = seṅdhiyā, q.v. s.m. A miner, house-breaker, burglar ( = seṅdh-mār).

ety: S. सङ्गरं; or श्टङ्ग+ रं and र+इका
def: The capsule of a legume, a pod, siliqua (seṅgrī is said to be particularly applied to the siliqua of the radish).
s.f. A ladder ( = sīṛhī).
adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to an army (senā), military; — a military man, a soldier; a sentinel, guard; a picket; a body of forces in array.
s.m. = saikṛā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit शत (śatá, “hundred”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-) + -𑀟- (-ḍa-) + -𑀓- (-ka-). Cognate with Punjabi ਸੈਂਕੜਾ (saiṅkṛā) / سَینکڑا (sainkṛā), Gujarati સેંકડો (seṅkḍo), Marathi शेकडा (śekḍā), शेकडो (śekḍo).

def: s.f. & adv. Anything obtained gratis or for nothing; a thing given to boot; — free of cost or charge, gratis, for nothing; to boot; — of no expense; gratuitously, causelessly
subs: or seṅt-meṅt, or saiṅt-maiṅt-meṅ, or saiṅt-meṅ, adv. = saiṅt.

def: adj. Forty-seven.

ety: ˚wāṅ = Prk. अमओ or अवँओ = S. तम+कः
def: adj. (f. ˚wīṅ), Forty-seventh.
s.m. Adjustment, arrangement, rectification, &c. (see saiṅtnā).
s.f. (dialec.) An iron spear, javelin, lance, dart.

ety: saiṅt, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. & adv. = saiṅt, q.v
adj. Thirty-seven.
adj. (f. ˚wīṅ), Thirty-seventh.

ety: saint˚ = Prk. समाहित्त(इ), fr. समाहित्त (Ap. Prk. सआहित्त or सआइत्त) = S. समाहित, p.p.p. of सम्+आ+धा
def: v.t. To set in order, put to rights, settle, dispose, arrange; to adjust, to rectify; to provide, supply; — to put away, lay by; to preserve carefully, take care of; to keep, tend; to husband; — to strike down, to kill, despatch, dispose of
subs: or saiṅt-ke rakhnā, v.t. To preserve, keep carefully, &c.
v.t. To lay by; to lay in; to provide, &c.

def: s.m. A soldier, sentinel, guard; — an army
subs: s.m. The surrounding of an army.

def: s.m. A soldier, sentinel, guard; — an army
subs: s.m. The surrounding of an army.

ety: act. part. of سأر or سئر 'to remain'
def: s.m. The rest, remainder; the whole.
part. & s.m. Going, walking, wandering; current; contingent; — tax, duty (levied on personal property), land-customs, octroi

ety: v.n. fr. سعر 'to light a fire'
def: s.f. Fire, hell-fire; name of a certain hell, or quarter of hell (destined for idolaters).

s.f. Moving about, strolling, stroll, ramble, walk, taking the air, airing, perambulation, excursion, tour, travels; recreation, amusement; scene, view, spectacle, landscape; perusal (of a book, &c.); a sally (of wit, &c.), a jest
subs: v.n. To take the air, to stroll, ramble, perambulate; to take amusement, to enjoy sights, to view or contemplate a beautiful landscape; to make an excursion, &c.; to read, peruse
s.f. Place of perambulation, walk, passage, thoroughfare; place of recreation; a place affording a good view or prospect
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَیر (sayr), from Arabic سَيْر (sayr).

def: s.m. A money-changer, &c. ( = ṣarrāf); an examiner, or a skilful judge, of coins, jewels, and precious stones; — a practiser of art or artifice or cunning, a deceiver, a cheat.
s.m. A money-changer, &c. ( = ṣarrāf); an examiner, or a skilful judge, of coins, jewels, and precious stones; — a practiser of art or artifice or cunning, a deceiver, a cheat.
s.m. pl. (of sāʼir, — barbarous), Taxes (on personal property), transit duties.
s.m. = سائس sāʼis, q.v. s.m. A groom, horse-keeper.
s.m. = sāʼīs, q.v. s.m. A groom, horse-keeper.
s.m. See سئيس saʼīs, and سائيس sāʼīs.

ety: act. part. of سوس 'to manage or tend beasts'
def: s.m. A groom, horse-keeper.
s.f. = سائسي sāʼisī, q.v. s.f. The business of a groom.
s.f. = sāʼīsī, q.v. s.f. The business of a groom.
s.f. = سئيسي saʼisī, and سائيسي sāʼīsī, q.v. s.f. The business of a groom.
s.f. The business of a groom.
s.m. Name of an agricultural tribe near Benares.
s.m. corr. of śaithilya, q.v.
s.m. Looseness, laxity; flaccidity; slackness, remissness; dilatoriness, inattention; weakness; cowardice smallness; relaxation of rule or of connexion.

v.t. = saiṅtnā, q.v. v.t. To set in order, put to rights, settle, dispose, arrange; to adjust, to rectify; to provide, supply; — to put away, lay by; to preserve carefully, take care of; to keep, tend; to husband; — to strike down, to kill, despatch, dispose of
subs: or saiṅt-ke rakhnā, v.t. To preserve, keep carefully, &c.
v.t. To lay by; to lay in; to provide, &c.

def: s.m. corr. of śaitya, q.v.
s.m. Coldness, cold, frigidity.
s.m. = شيو śaiva, q.v.

adj. Relating or belonging to the god Śiva; — s.m. Name of one of the three great divisions of modern Hindū sects (the other two being Vaishṇavas and Śāktas); a worshipper of Śiva, a Śivite; — the plant Vallisneria octandra.
s.m. = saiwār, or siwār, q.v.
s.m. = śaivāl, q.v.
adj. (f. -īṅ), The hundredth.

ety: intens. n. fr. صيد; see ṣaid
def: s.m. A sportsman, hunter, fowler, fisher; (poet.) one who captivates, a ravisher of hearts (syn. shikarī).

ety: ṣaiyād, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Hunting, fowling, shooting, trapping or snaring game, &c.
s.m. A great or frequent traveller, an itinerant; a pilgrim; a travelled man; — adj. Travelling, peregrinating, itinerant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَیَّاح (sayyāh), from Arabic سَيَّاح (sayyāḥ).

def: s.f. Travelling, journeying; journey, voyage; pilgrimage.
adj. Flowing or running much, flowing rapidly, rapid.

ety: for A. سيالة saiyālat, fem. of saiyāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A torrent; a body or volume (of anything) poured out or forth.
s.m. = sāʼīṅ, or sāṅʼīṅ, qq.v. s.m. = سانئي sāṅʼī, q.v. s.m.f. Second; — a second; match, equal
subs: adv. A second or another time, secondly

ety: v.n. fr. سير 'to go; to journey,' &c.
def: s.m. A journeyer, traveller, wanderer; a person taking the air, or one travelling for amusement.

ety: for A. سيارة, saiyārat, fem. of saiyār, q.v.
def: s.m. A planet (P. plur. saiyāragān)
subs: The seven planets (the Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سیاره (sayyāra), from Arabic سَيَّارَة (sayyāra), itself an ellipsis of كَوْكَب سَيَّار (kawkab sayyār, “wandering star”).

ety: for A. ثيبة s̤aiyibat, for s̤aiyīb, orig. s̤awīb
def: s.f. A young woman who has consummated her marriage, or one that is not a virgin.
s.m. A lord, ohief, prince; — any descendant of Mohammad, (esp.) a descendant of Husain (who was the grandson of Moham mad)
subs: 'Chief of the apostles, or prophets,' Mohammad
adj. & s.m. Born of a Saiyid; — a Saiyid's son.

ety: for A. سيدة saiyidat, fem. of سيد
def: s.f. The wife or daughter of a Saiyid; a lady, a princess.
s.f. A woman of the Saiyid caste; the wife or daughter of a saiyid.
s.m. An appellation of Africans; a negro.
s.m. (but. gen. f. in Hindī), Preparation; harness, accoutrements, fittings (cf. sāz).
s.m. The teak-tree, Tectona grandis (syn. sāgūn); — the plane-tree.

ety: S. सज्जा; or fr. sajnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Preparation; dress, ornament, decoration; appearance, shape
subs: s.f. Preparation and appearance; figure; adorning, ornamentation; grace, elegance.

ety: inf. n. of سجع 'to speak, &c. in rhymed prose'
def: s.m. Rhythmical speech or language, rhymed prose, rhyme, cadence; metre; poesy
subs: s.m. One who speaks rhythmically; a rhymer; an eloquent speaker.

def: s.f. corr. of sazā, q.v. s.m. (local), corr. of sājhā, q.v. s.m. Association, company (in trade), partnership; relation, connection; — share (in a company); — adj. Joint
subs: (-kā), Partnership to be dissolved; connection to be severed; — sājhā ćhūṭnā, Dissolution of partnership
v.n. To form an association or a company; to enter into partnership
or sājhe-meṅ, adv. In partnership, jointly
adv. As a partner or sharer in, with the eye of a partner, as if in co-parcenary.

def: adj. Wide awake, watchful, vigilant, circumspect, cautious, careful.
s.f. Preparation, &c. ( = sajāwaṭ, q.v.); — price paid for accoutrements, or for belts and scabbards, &c.
Possessing or containing water, filled with or abounding in water, watery, wet, moist, humid.

ety: Prk. सज्जणो; S. सज्जनः, i.e. सत्+जनः
def: s.m. Lover, paramour, sweetheart, gallant; husband (cf. sajjan).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡 (sajjaṇa), from Sanskrit सज्जन (sajjana, “good man”), from सत् (sát) + जन (jána).

def: s.m. Preparing, dressing, decorating, adorning.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡 (sajjaṇa), from Sanskrit सज्जन (sajjana, “good man”), from सत् (sát) + जन (jána).

def: v.t. To cause (a person) to prepare (a thing); to arrange, prepare, assort, methodize; to decorate, adorn; to mend
subs: v.t. To put into a state of preparation; to arrange, order, to set out; to adorn.

ety: said to be a Greek word arabicised
def: s.m. A mirror, a Chinese mirror; — spectacles; adj. Pure, clear.

ety: sajā(nā) + Prk. एअव्वं and एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्यं, and (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Preparation, arrangement, order; adorning, decoration, ornament; contrivance.
s.m.f. Of the same sort or species, of the same tribe or caste or race; — a fellow-clansman; a compeer; &c.
s.m.f. Of the same sort or species, of the same tribe or caste or race; — a fellow-clansman; a compeer; &c.
adj. = sa-jāt, q.v. s.m.f. Of the same sort or species, of the same tribe or caste or race; — a fellow-clansman; a compeer; &c.
s.f. Dressing, decking or adorning (oneself).

ety: sajā(nā) + Prk. एअव्वं and एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्यं, and (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Preparation, arrangement, order; adorning, decoration, ornament; contrivance.
s.f. (pl. of سجية sajīyat, v.n. fr. سجو 'to cover or conceal by darkness'), Nature, natural disposition or temper; faculty, quality.

ety: Prk. सज्झओ; S. साह्य, or सह्य (rt. सह्)+कः
def: s.m. Association, company (in trade), partnership; relation, connection; — share (in a company); — adj. Joint
subs: (-kā), Partnership to be dissolved; connection to be severed; — sājhā ćhūṭnā, Dissolution of partnership
v.n. To form an association or a company; to enter into partnership
or sājhe-meṅ, adv. In partnership, jointly
adv. As a partner or sharer in, with the eye of a partner, as if in co-parcenary.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. sājhin, or sājhan), Assistant, helpful; — associate, colleague; partner; coparcener; shareholder
subs: v.n. To be or become a partner, &c. ( = sājhā karnā, q.v.).

ety: act. part. of سجد 'to bend oneself down to the ground,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Prostrate in adoration; — one who prostrates himself in adoration or prayer, an adorer, a worshipper.
adj. (f. -ī), Well-shaped, graceful, handsome; well-dressed; decorated.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having life, endowed with life, living, alive; — a living creature.
adj. corr. of sa-jīv, q.v.

s.m. = sanjīvan, q.v. s.m. Bringing to life, animating; re-animating, life-restoring; — the act of living together; — a cluster of four houses, a quadrangle; — name of one of the divisions of the infernal regions.

ety: for A. سجية sajīyat, v.n. fr. سجو 'to cover or conceal by darkness,' &c.
def: s.m. Nature, disposition, temper (see the pl. sajāyā, which is the more common form).
s.f. An armed female, &c. (see sajja); — dress; decoration; apparatus, equipment, equipage, harness; armour, mail.
adj. Right (arm or hand).
adj. Covered, clothed, dressed, accoutred; ornamented, decorated; prepared, ready; armed, fortified; — s.m. An armed person, &c.

ety: intens. n. fr. سجد 'to prostrate oneself'
def: adj. & s.m. Prostrating oneself much or frequently (in prayer), bowing the head in adoration, adoring; — one who bows in adoration, &c.

ety: for A. سجادة sajjādat; see sajjād
def: s.m. A carpet or mat on which a Mohammadan prostrates himself (and stands and sits) in prayer; place of adoration; mosque; altar
subs: s.m. lit. 'Sitting upon the prayer-carpet'; a Mohammadan priest (when saying prayers); any religious ascetic of the Mohammadan faith; — the spiritual superior of a mosque or religious endowment (as distinguished from the Mutawallī or secular superintendent).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.
s.m. Fastening on (clothes), dressing; putting on armour, arming, preparing; — a guard, sentry, picquet; a ferry (syn. ghāṭ).
s.f. = sajanī, q.v.

s.m. = sajjan, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.
s.f. The act of dressing; decoration; ornamenting or caparisoning an elephant.
s.f. A virtuous woman; a respectable woman; a mistress, sweetheart; a female friend (syn. sahelī).
s.f. Natron, impure carbonate of soda, a kind of mineral alkali (used in India instead of soap for cleaning linen, &c.; syn. khār)
adj. & s.m. = saṅjhlā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), The third (of four brothers, &c.); of the third rank or degree; — the third of four brothers.

ety: sāj˚ = Prk. सज्ज(इ) = S. सज्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. संज्
def: v.t. To prepare, to fit out; to dress, decorate, bedeck; to regulate; — v.n. ( = sajnā), To be prepared, &c.; to be accoutred; — to become, fit, suit.
v.t. To arrange, adjust, rectify; to prepare, dress, accoutre; to ornament; to ornament, adorn.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), = sa-jnānī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessed of knowledge, knowing, intelligent, wise, sagacious; — an intelligent man, a wise man, &c.
s.f. Intelligence, sagacity, wisdom.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessed of knowledge, knowing, intelligent, wise, sagacious; — an intelligent man, a wise man, &c.
s.f. corr. of sva-jnāti, q.v. s.f. One's own kindred or kin; — s.m.f. Kinsman, relation.
part. (f. -ā), Known, apprehended, perceived.
s.m.f. Possessed of youth, in the prime of youth, in the enjoyment of youth, youthful; — one who is experiencing the season or the condition of youth.
v.t. To cause to be prepared, to have or get prepared, to have arranged; to have rectified, &c.
s.f. = śayyā or sej, qq.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = sahajnā, q.v.; — 3˚ s.m. corr. of sajīya, q.v.
s.f. corr. of śayyā, q.v.
s.f. The leg (from the ankle to the knee), the shank; — trunk, shaft, bole (of a tree); stalk (of a herb)
subs: s.f. lit. 'Bride's leg'; a sort of sweet bread.

def: s.m. Pot-herbs, greens ( = sāg, q.v.)
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. = شك śaka or śak, q.v. s.m. Era, epoch (generally applied to the era of Sālivāhan, reckoning from about 78 ¼ of the Christian era)
subs: v.n. To establish an era; (met.) to distinguish oneself by heroic actions
s.m. A king who has established an era ( = śākeśvar).

def: s.f. Ability, capacity, strength, power, authority.
s.f. corr. of شك shakk, q.v. adj. Difficult, hard, distressing, grievous, burdensome, afflicting, troublesome, severe, perplexing, embarrassing, wearisome, &c.
s.m. Era, epoch (generally applied to the era of Sālivāhan, reckoning from about 78 ¼ of the Christian era)
subs: v.n. To establish an era; (met.) to distinguish oneself by heroic actions
s.m. A king who has established an era ( = śākeśvar).

def: s.m. Might, power, main.
wety: Either from Classical Persian سکا (sakā) or a modern learned borrowing from an ancient Indo-Iranian language referring to the same nomadic peoples.

def: s.m. Any edible leaf, or fruit, or root, or herb; a vegetable, pot-herb, greens ( = sāg, q.v.); — the teak tree, Tectona grandis; — power, strength, energy, force; — name of the sixth of the seven dvīpas or divisions of the world (surrounded by the white or milky sea); — an era, a period (usually dating from the reign of some celebrated prince; and most generally applied to the era of Śālivāhan)
subs: (˚ka+īś˚), s.m. An epithet of any prince (as Yudhishṭhir, Vikramāditya, or Śālivāhan) who has established an era, or after whom an era is named.

def: s.m. A sovereign or prince who gives his name to an era (esp. applied to Śālivāhan); — an era, epoch (esp. that of the monarch Śālivāhan, commencing 76 or 78 years after the Christian era); — name of a particular tribe or race of people (regarded by some as the followers or the descendants of Śālivāhan)
subs: (˚ka+ab˚), s.m. A year of the śaka era.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَک (šak), borrowed from Arabic شَكّ (šakk).

def: adj. All, entire, the whole, every, each and all
subs: s.m. lit. 'Dwelling in all hearts'; an epithet of Vishnu.

def: s.m. Name of a school of the Rig-veda (supposed to have been founded by the elder Śākalya); — s.f. A mixture of sesasum seeds, barley, ghī, coarse sugar, fruits, &c., used by Hindūs in oblations to the gods.
s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
s.f. = śākal, q.v. s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
s.m. pl. Name of a tribe inhabiting Bhārat-varsha; the followers of the Śākala school of the Veda.

ety: for A. ثقالة, inf. n. of ثقل 'to be heavy'
def: s.f. Heaviness, weight; heavy load, indigestion ( = s̤iql).
wety: From Classical Persian ثقالت (saqālat), from Arabic verbal noun ثَقَالَة (ṯaqāla), from verb root ثَقُلَ (ṯaqula, “to be heavy”).

def: adv. Betimes, early in the morning, at dawn.
s.f. Early morning, dawn ( = sakāl); — adv. Early in the morning ( = sakāle).
v.t. = sakārnā, q.v. v.t. To accept, or endorse as accepted (a bill)
subs: s.m. Accepter, or endorser (of a bill)

def: s.m. Totality, entireness, completeness; the whole, all.
corr. of sākalya, q.v. s.m. Totality, entireness, completeness; the whole, all.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Contemed satisfied, gratified, happy; full of love, loving; in love; sensual, lascivious; — done with a desire of future reward or fruition; — one who has attained his desire, a contented person; a lover, &c.
adj. Engaged, occupied, busy.
s.f. Name of a city (supposed to be the modern Sāmbhar in Ajmer).
s.m. A kind of fossil salt brought from a lake in the vicinity of the town Sāmbhar.
fem. of sāqī, q.v.
s.m. A dwelling, residence (syn. maskan).

ety: prob. fr. S. शुक्, rt. सुच्
def: v.n. To be sorrowful; to grieve, mourn, repine; to be wearied or languid.
(for A. سکنة sakanat, pl. of sākin), s.m. pl. Inhabitants, residents.
s.f. pl. (of saknat), Rests, pauses, &c.

ety: rel. n. fr. A. sakan, q.v.
def: adj. Habitable.
s.m. The skink, Lacerta scincus.
adj. Having thorns, thorny, prickly; troublesome, perilous; — s.m. The aquatic plant Vallisneria.

ety: v.n. fr. سقر 'to burn or scorch'
def: s.m. Hell
s.m. = sa-kāl, q.v. s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
s.m. Acceptance (of a hunḍī or bill), endorsement (of a bill) noting acceptance.
adj. = sakal, q.v. s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
adj. Possessed of form or shape, of definite figure, figurate.
Charge for accepting and cashing (a bill of exchange).
Charge for accepting and cashing (a bill of exchange).
adj. Having some cause, originating from a cause.

ety: pl. of sakrat, v.n. fr. سکر; see sukr
def: s.f. Fainting, insensibility, agony (of death)
subs: s.f. Insensibility attendant upon death, pangs or agony of death.

def: s.m. Great-grandfather, or great-great-grandfather (on the father's side).
adv. = sakāle, q.v. adv. Betimes, early in the morning, at dawn.
s.f. = sakālī or sa-kāl; — adv. = sakāle; qq.v. s.f. Early morning, dawn ( = sakāl); — adv. Early in the morning ( = sakāle).
adj. (in Gram.), Having an object, transitive.
v.t. To accept, or endorse as accepted (a bill)
subs: s.m. Accepter, or endorser (of a bill)

ety: sakaṛ˚, &c. = Prk. संकड्ढ(इ), fr. संकड्ढ = S. सङ्क्टष्ट p.p.p. of rt. सम्+कृष्
def: v.n. To draw together, draw in or up, to become contracted, to contract, to become tightened or tight; to dwindle; to shrink, shrivel, to curl up, to wrinkle; to be gathered together, be collected, to gather, collect; to be swept up or together
subs: or sikaṛ-jānā, &c. v.n. Intens. of and = sikuṛnā.

def: s.m. Great-grandfather, or great-great-grandfather (on the mother's side).
adj. Having pity, commiserating, pitiful, tender, compassionate.

ety: v.n. fr. سقط 'to fall or drop,' &c.
def: s.m. What is dropped, or thrown away; anything worthless or useless, or held in mean estimation; refuse, rubbish; merchandise of a perishable quality (either spoilt, or spoiling); — an error, blunder, mistake, defect; anything disgraceful or shameful; improper or evil speech, abusive language, abuse
subs: v.n. To drop, to die (applied to animals); to be dropped (as the young of an animal), to miscarry.

def: s.f. Power, strength, ability, &c. (see śakti)
s.m. A cart (cf. ćhakṛā).
s.m. A strait, difficulty, pressing distress; — a Hindū festival, the festival of the birthday of Ganeś
subs: s.f. The festival of the birthday of Ganeś.

def: adj. Bad, vile; — s.m. The small tree Trophis aspera.

s.m. A cart ( = ćhakṛā); a cart-load; an implement for preparing grain; a form of military array resembling a wedge; the figure of a cart formed by the five stars composing the lunar asterism Rohiṇī (the fourth mansion of the Hindū zodiac); — śakaṭāsur (˚ṭa+as˚) or śakaṭ, s.m. Name of a daitya or demon slain by the infant Krishṇ.
adj. corr. of شکت saḵẖt, q.v. s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.
s.f. A small cart; a child's cart, a toy-cart.
s.f. A small cart; a child's cart, a toy-cart.
s.m. A cold bath, a cistern for cold water for washing or bathing, a bath-room.
s.m. A cold bath, a cistern for cold water for washing or bathing, a bath-room.
s.m. A kind of iron; — an iron instrument or weapon; a sword.
v.n. & t. = sakeṛnā, q.v.
s.f. A drawing together, contraction, shrinking, &c. ( = sikoṛ).

ety: sakeṛ˚ = S. सङ्क्टष्ट, p.p.p. of सम्+कृष
def: v.n. = sukaṛnā or sikuṛnā, q.v.; — v.t. = sakoṛnā or sikoṛnā, q.v.
v.n. & t. = sakelnā, q.v.
adj. Narrow, strait, tight, small; — s.f. Straits, difficulty, distress, want
subs: v.t. To confine, to narrow, &c. ( = saketnā).

def: s.f. = saket, q.v. adj. Narrow, strait, tight, small; — s.f. Straits, difficulty, distress, want
subs: v.t. To confine, to narrow, &c. ( = saketnā).

def: v.t. To draw together, to narrow, confine, to brace up, tighten.

ety: inf. n. of سقف 'to make a roof,' &c.
def: s.f. Roof, ceiling; canopy; — the sky; — a platform.
s.f. Beam, rafter.
s.f.m. Evidence (of a witness), testimony; credibility; credit, trust, mercantile credit; name, reputation, honourable character.
s.m. Friend, dear friend ( = sakhi); — the tree Mimosa catechu
subs: s.m. The friend referred to, the aforesaid friend.

def: s.f. A branch; genealogy (in these senses, com. sākhā); a season, time; crop, harvest; — sākhā-mrig, s.m. lit. 'Branch animal'; a monkey; a squirrel
subs: (sākhā+uć˚), s.m. Relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.

def: s.m. 1˚ = sākā, q.v.; — 2˚ Quarrel, fight
subs: adj. & s.m. (colloq.) Quarrelsome; — a quarrelsome person.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Credible, trustworthy; creditable; of good reputation, honourable, respectable.

ety: inf. n. of سخو or سخو 'to be liberal'
def: s.f. = next, q.v.

s.f. A branch, bough; an arm; a party, sect; a division, section, subdivision; a subdivision of the Vedas according to the different schools and redactions of the holy writings; — any part of an animal devoid of sensibility (as a horn, &c.)
subs: (˚khā+uć˚), s.m. The relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.

def: s.f. A branch; genealogy (in these senses, com. sākhā); a season, time; crop, harvest; — sākhā-mrig, s.m. lit. 'Branch animal'; a monkey; a squirrel
subs: (sākhā+uć˚), s.m. Relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.

def: s.m. Friend, associate, companion
subs: s.m. pl. A number or host of companions.

def: s.m. corr. of شخض shaḵẖṣ, q.v. s.m. A man's self or person; a person, being, body, individual
subs: s.m. The person in possession, the possessor
s.m. A single person, an individual; an unassisted person.

ety: for A. سخاوة, inf. n. of سخو
def: s.f. Liberality, generosity, munificence.
s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
s.m. (f.?), A tree.
s.m. (f. -ī) = sakhā, q.v. s.f. A branch; genealogy (in these senses, com. sākhā); a season, time; crop, harvest; — sākhā-mrig, s.m. lit. 'Branch animal'; a monkey; a squirrel
subs: (sākhā+uć˚), s.m. Relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.
wety: First attested in c. 1625 as Middle Hindi سکھی (sakhī), borrowed from Sanskrit सखी (sakhī).

def: adj. & s.m. Having branches, branched, branching, ramifying, &c., belonging to any branch, or school, or sect, &c.; — a tree; a Veda (as having various branches or schools); — one who practises the injunctions of any particular branch of the Vedas, a follower of any school or sect.
adj. & s.m. Liberal, bountiful, generous; — a liberal or bountiful person.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سخی (saxī), from Arabic سَخِيّ (saḵiyy).

def: s.f. A woman's friend or companion, a female friend or confidante; a handmaid, an abigail; — a class of mendicants who dress like women; an individual of that class; an effeminate man
subs: s.f. A female friend or confidante
s.m. A kind of verse in the beginning of a lāʼonī, q.v.
wety: First attested in c. 1625 as Middle Hindi سکھی (sakhī), borrowed from Sanskrit सखी (sakhī).

def: corr. of सखि sakhi, q.v. s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
s.f. = S سکهتو सखित्व sakhitva, s.m. Friendship, intimacy.
s.m. = S سکهتا सखिता sakhitā, s.f. Friendship, intimacy.
s.f. = sakhī, q.v. s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
s.m. = sakrā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Bad, impure, polluted (food); — impure food (cf. S. sakaṭānna); unfried food (regarded as not proper to be eaten outside of the rasoʼī, q.v.).

ety: v.n. of sāḵẖtan; sāḵẖ = Zend śać = S. शक्; tan = S. aff. तं
def: s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.

ety: prob. Zend śakhta, rt. śac = S. शक्त, rt. शक्; but said to be another form of sanjīda, q.v.
def: adj. Hard, stiff, rigid, firm, fast; strong, solid; tight; constipated; obdurate, obstinate; stingy, sordid; wretched; difficult, arduous, troublesome; painful, grievous; severe, intense, vehement, violent; great, gross, excessive, extreme, egregious; austere, stern, harsh; very cruel, fell; — adv. Very, intensely, violently, severely, excessively, extremely, &c.
subs: adj. Of austere countenance, stern-faced, sour-looking
(-se), To reproach, scold, chide, to rail (at)
adj. & s.m. Harsh or unpleasant of speech; saying unpleasant things; — one who speaks harshly or says unpleasant things
s.f. Harshness or unpleasantness of language; abuse
adj. Holding fast (an animal of chase)
s.f. Extortion, exaction, taking by force.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سخت (saxt), from Middle Persian 𐭮𐭠𐭤𐭨 (saxt), 𐭮𐭤𐭨 (saxt).

ety: perf. part. of saḵẖtan; see sāḵẖt
def: part. adj. Made, formed; artificial, counterfeited, fictitious, false, feigned; surreptitious
subs: and sāḵẖta-pardākhta, part. adj. & s.m. Done and effected; anything done or had done, act, deed
adj. & adv. Natural, unprepared, unaffected; simple; unpremeditated, impromptu, extempore; without preparation, spontaneously, on the spur of the moment.

def: s.f. Make, &c. ( = sāḵẖt, q.v.); art, design; effort; machination. s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.

s.f. Hardness, stiffness, rigidity, firmness; tightness; stinginess; obduracy, obstinacy; intenseness, intensity, vehemence, severity; harshness, asperity; sternness, austereness; violence, atrocity; cruelty; grievance, hardship; adversity, indigence, distress, difficulty, evil, calamity
subs: (-se), To use force or violence (towards), treat with harshness or severity, &c.

def: s.m. = sāgūn and sāṅkhū, qq.v. s.m. Omen, augury (cf. shugūn).
s.m. Friendship, intimacy; equality.

adv. &c. = sākshāt, q.v. adv. Before the eyes, in sight (of), in the presence (of), before; eye to eye, face to face; visibly, evidently, openly, manifestly; as, like, like as; — adj. Visible, evident, open, manifest, conspicuous; precisely like, exact, true, identical, the very same; — s.m. Interview, meeting
subs: adj. Causing to be visibly present, making evident, proving.

def: s.m. 1˚ = sākhī or sākshī; — 2˚ = sākhī or śākhī; qq.v. s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.

ety: for A. ساقٍ sāqiṉ, act. part. of سقي 'to give (water, &c.
def: s.m. One who gives to drink, cup-bearer, page; (in India) one who gets ready and brings the ḥuqqa
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَاقِی (sāqī), from Arabic سَاقٍ (sāqin).

def: (act. part. of ثقب 'to pierce'), adj. lit. 'Penetrating'; shining brightly, glistening, gleaming very brightly, brilliant, splendid; famous, exalted, sublime, high.
adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous; hard to be borne, onerous, burdensome, oppressive, afflictive; indigestible; slow, sluggish, indolent, lazy, phlegmatic; dull, torpid, inactive.
v.t. To bring together, to collect, &c.; — to push away.

r: saqilāt̤, siqlāt̤, suqlāt̤, sijillāt̤, sijlāt̤, saqillāt, saqilāt, saqallāt, saqalāt
def: s.f. (?) Scarlet cloth.

r: saqillāt, saqilāt, saqallāt, saqalāt, saqilāt̤, siqlāt̤, suqlāt̤, sijillāt̤, sijlāt̤
def: s.f. (?) Scarlet cloth.

ety: prob. fr. S. सङ्कीर्ण, rt. कृ with सम्
def: v.n. To gather, collect, accumulate, to convene; to purse; to shrink, &c. ( = sikuṛnā).
adj. Diseased, disordered, sick, ailing; infirm, weak; unsound, faulty, defective.

ety: act. part. of سکن 'to be still, or stationary,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. In a state of rest, quiet, calm, tranquil, peaceable; quiescent (letter); inhabiting, dwelling; — a quiescent letter, a consonant not followed by a vowel; — an inhabitant, resident.
s.f. A kind of inferior divinity (or female demon or fairy) attendant chiefly on Śiva and Durgā.

ety: act. part. of سقط 'to fall, drop,' &c.
def: part. adj. Falling, dropping; fallen, dropped; unavailable; out of use, obsolete; lost; degraded, depraved, vile, contemptible
subs: v.t. To drop, to throw down; to cast; to reject
v.n. To drop, fall; to abate. to lapse; to be dropped; to become obsolete; to be fallen, be lost, degraded.

ety: act. part. of سکت 'to be silent'
def: adj. Silent, quiet, mute, at rest.
part. Capable of being accomplished, capable, able.
s.m. pl. of sāqī, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. سقي 'to give to drink'
def: s.m. Water-carrier; one who gives to drink, a cup-bearer ( = sāqī); waterer (of plants, &c.); — a pelican ( = murg-ě-saqqā, — so called because it draws water for itself).

ety: v.n. fr. سكّ 'to make firm'; 'to clamp,' &c.
def: s.m. (orig.) 'A stamper of money'; a maker of knives, a cutler.
